content: Hi, I'm Dakota, and I'm an aerospace engineer at SpaceX. I'm really passionate about calligraphy and knitting, and I try to make time for them whenever I can.
Calligraphy and knitting are great hobbies! They're so creative and relaxing. What do you like most about them?

I love the precision and attention to detail required for calligraphy, and the creativity and self-expression that comes with knitting. I've also been learning origami to help me relax and express my creativity.
Origami is a great way to challenge yourself and create something beautiful. Have you thought about how you'll balance your work as an aerospace engineer with your hobbies?
Yeah, it can be tough, but I try to prioritize my time and make sure I have some time for myself each day. I've also been cooking Thai food and experimenting with new recipes.
Thai food is delicious! What's your favorite dish to cook?
I love making pad thai and green curry. I've also been thinking about getting a vintage car, maybe a 1969 Mustang, to restore and drive on the weekends.
A 1969 Mustang is a classic! That sounds like a fun project. Have you thought about how you'll restore it or maintain it?
Yeah, I've been doing some research and talking to friends who are into vintage cars. I'm thinking about joining a local car club to learn more.
That's a great idea! Joining a community of like-minded people can be really helpful and fun. You might learn some new skills and make some great connections.

I'm also planning to get married next year, and I'm thinking about how to incorporate my hobbies into the wedding. Maybe I'll even make some origami decorations or have a vintage car as a getaway car.
That sounds like a wonderful idea! Incorporating your hobbies into your wedding will make it even more special and meaningful. Congratulations on your upcoming wedding!
Thanks! I'm really excited. I've also been learning Rust programming to help me with my work at SpaceX and to build some personal projects.
Rust is a great language! It's so versatile and powerful. What kind of projects are you thinking about building with Rust?
I'm thinking about building some tools to help with data analysis and visualization. Maybe I'll even create a program to help me with my calligraphy and knitting projects.
That sounds like a great idea! Using Rust to build tools for your hobbies will be a fun and challenging project.
Yeah, I'm looking forward to it. I'll be sure to share my progress with my friends and family and get their feedback.
I'm sure they'll be excited to see what you're working on. It's great that you're pursuing your passions and interests outside of work.
